schema_version: v0 — skill-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Session open: read the latest slate from CairnCommons/slates/ and restore the resumption point (in-flight work, next direction, open threads).
why: Sessions are bounded by context limits; continuity must live in a durable artifact, not in memory of a conversation that won't survive the boundary. Loading the slate is the fifth feedback loop's open-half (loadslate/saveslate — session continuity), and it keeps the resolver from re-deriving a settled resumption point (Law 1).
invoke: /loadslate  (at session open; or /loadslate <name> for a specific slate)
traces_to: Law 1 (don't re-derive the settled resumption point)
Law 3 (a slate is a snapshot to verify, not ground truth)
the map is authoritative over memory of the conversation (MAP.md)
owner: the skills charter gates writes; Akien ratifies changes (signature gate)
proof: skill node → done only when cairnmap recompiles green (derivation gate). IOU until cairnmap exists — tracked in proofs/.
provenance: converged in the founding design conversation (MAP.md 'Workflow + starting skills', 2026-07-15).
